verdicts.issue: Key year 2019 vs arXiv preprint year 2018 (1805.06085, May 2018). Within 1-year preprint tolerance per bib-check rule.

Year drift: key claims Coleman 2017 (NIPS ML Systems Workshop) but body has no year field, only date = {2019-07-25}. Bib content describes the 2019 ACM SIGOPS Operating Systems Review reprint (DOI 10.1145/3352020.3352024), not the original 2017 workshop paper. Either rename key to coleman2019dawnbench (and update .qmd citations) or replace bib metadata with the original 2017 NIPS workshop reference.

suggested_fix.note: If renaming the key, update all .qmd files that cite [@coleman2017dawnbench] to [@coleman2019dawnbench] (link-propagation requirement). Alternatively, keep the 2017 key and replace bib body with the original NIPS ML Systems Workshop 2017 entry.

verdicts.issue: Key year 2016 but BinaryConnect was published at NeurIPS 2015 (vol 28 covers 2015). Within 1-year tolerance.
Duplicate of crankshaw2017clipper (same paper, same authors, same venue). Two keys for the same NSDI'17 paper is wasteful but not technically broken. Consider consolidating in a future cleanup.
Author field has only 'Curnow, H. J.' but the original Curnow & Wichmann 1976 Whetstone benchmark paper has TWO authors: H. J. Curnow and B. A. Wichmann. Missing co-author Wichmann.

Key claims dally 2020 ('Hardware for Deep Learning') but body cites the 2023 Hot Chips 35 talk (DOI 10.1109/hcs59251.2023.10254716, date = {2023-08-27}). The key year 2020 does not match the actual content year 2023 (3-year drift, exceeds 1-year tolerance).

suggested_fix.note: Rename key and update all .qmd files citing [@dally_hardware_2020] to [@dally2023hardware] (link-propagation requirement).

verdicts.issue: Year drift: key claims Dastin 2018 (original Reuters article date) but body has no year field, only date = {2022-03-16}. Bib content describes the 2022 reprint as a chapter in 'Ethics of Data and Analytics' (DOI 10.1201/9781003278290-44), not the 2018 Reuters article. Same pattern as angwin2016machine flagged in prior audit.

suggested_fix.note: Either keep key dastin2018amazon and replace bib metadata with the original 2018 Reuters URL (https://www.reuters.com/article/world/insight-amazon-scraps-secret-ai-recruiting-tool-that-showed-bias-against-women-idUSKCN1MK0AG/), or rename key to dastin2022amazon to match the 2022 reprint and update .qmd citations.

verdicts.issue: Author list corrupted. The actual Davies et al. 2021 'Advancing Neuromorphic Computing With Loihi' paper (Proc. IEEE 109(5):911-934) has authors: Mike Davies, Andreas Wild, Garrick Orchard, Yulia Sandamirskaya, Gabriel A. Fonseca Guerra, Prasad Joshi, Philipp Plank, Sumedh R. Risbud. Bib has wrong/fabricated names: 'Orber, Garrick' (should be Orchard, Garrick), 'Sandhu, Sumit', 'Chase, Jonathan', 'Dey, Shyam', 'Steffens, Wilfried', 'Agarwal, Anup' do not appear on the paper. Silent corruption per bib-check rule §5.

suggested_fix.author: Davies, Mike and Wild, Andreas and Orchard, Garrick and Sandamirskaya, Yulia and Fonseca Guerra, Gabriel A. and Joshi, Prasad and Plank, Philipp and Risbud, Sumedh R.

verdicts.issue: Year drift and missing year field: key claims Dean 2004 (original OSDI paper) but body has no year field, only date = {2008-01}. Bib content describes the 2008 CACM reprint (DOI 10.1145/1327452.1327492), not the 2004 OSDI proceedings paper. Also, booktitle = {OSDI} but journal = {Communications of the ACM} are inconsistent (CACM is not OSDI).

suggested_fix.note: Either keep key dean2004mapreduce and replace bib metadata with the original 2004 OSDI '04 entry (USENIX Association, https://www.usenix.org/legacy/publications/library/proceedings/osdi04/), or rename key to dean2008mapreduce to match the CACM reprint and update .qmd citations. Add explicit year field.

verdicts.issue: Author field abbreviated as 'Huang, Yanping and others'. Per rule §4a, full author list preferred where canonically known. Real authors: Huang, Yanping; Cheng, Youlong; Bapna, Ankur; Firat, Orhan; Chen, Dehao; Chen, Mia; Lee, HyoukJoong; Ngiam, Jiquan; Le, Quoc V.; Wu, Yonghui; Chen, Zhifeng. Also missing pages (103-112) and url.

Key year 2015 but date = {2016-02-18}. The book was published by Basic Books in September 2015; the Choice Reviews entry is from Feb 2016. Within 1-year tolerance, but consider replacing the Choice Reviews crossref with the actual book reference.
Duplicate of fastai_website (same author, year, URL). Consider consolidating in a future cleanup.
Duplicate of fastai (same author, year, URL). Consider consolidating in a future cleanup.
Key year 2021 (preprint year) vs JMLR publication year 2022. Within 1-year tolerance per bib-check rule (preprint-vs-conference gap is silent).

Key year 2015 (original NeurIPS workshop paper) vs body date = {2019} (the 'Automated Machine Learning' Springer book chapter). 4-year drift exceeds 1-year tolerance. Either rename or restore original 2015 NeurIPS reference.

suggested_fix.note: If keeping key Feurer2015, replace metadata with the original NeurIPS 2015 reference: 'Efficient and Robust Automated Machine Learning', NIPS 2015, https://papers.nips.cc/paper/2015/hash/11d0e6287202fced83f79975ec59a3a6-Abstract.html. Otherwise rename to Feurer2019 and update citations.

verdicts.issue: Could not verify this paper exists. Title 'The 8087 Numeric Data Processor' attributed to 'Fisher, Lawrence D.' in IEEE Computer 14(7) 1981 with DOI 10.1109/MC.1981.1653991 does not resolve (DOI returns 404). The canonical 8087 paper is John F. Palmer's 'The Intel 8087 numeric data processor' (1980 NCC, DOI 10.1145/1500518.1500674); Bruce Ravenel and John Palmer were the architects. No record of a Fisher 1981 IEEE Computer paper on the 8087 found in 3+ searches. Likely a fabricated/hallucinated reference.

suggested_fix.author: Palmer, John F.
suggested_fix.title: The Intel 8087 Numeric Data Processor
suggested_fix.venue: Proceedings of the May 19-22, 1980, National Computer Conference (AFIPS '80)
suggested_fix.note: Replace fabricated Fisher entry with the canonical Palmer 1980 paper. Update all .qmd files citing [@fisher_8087_1981] to [@palmer1980intel8087] (link-propagation requirement).

verdicts.issue: Two problems. (1) Key year 2024 vs body year 2023 (paper appeared at NeurIPS 2023 / arXiv April 2023) -- key year drift (1 year, borderline).
